articles.title: Introduction
articles.subtitle: Getting started with system design
articles.description: Learn what system design is, why it matters, and how to practice it effectively. This introduction covers the fundamentals you need to know before diving into system design interviews and real-world engineering challenges.
articles.author: System Design Sandbox

tableOfContents.title: What kinds of system design questions are there?
Why should I learn system design?
Why should I use this tool?

articles.title: System Design Structure
articles.subtitle: The steps to follow when designing a system
articles.description: Learn the structured approach to system design interviews. This guide covers functional requirements, non-functional requirements (availability, latency, reliability, consistency), API design, database modeling, and high-level design.
articles.author: System Design Sandbox

articles.title: How to Calculate Throughput & Database Size
articles.subtitle: A practical cheat sheet for estimating storage and throughput in system design interviews
articles.description: Learn how to calculate storage requirements and throughput for system design interviews. This guide covers the golden rule (2^10 ≈ 10^3), step-by-step storage calculations, throughput estimation, and a quick reference cheat sheet for interview success.
articles.author: System Design Sandbox

articles.title: Databases & Caching
articles.subtitle: Understanding when to use databases vs caches, and SQL vs NoSQL
articles.description: Learn the fundamental differences between databases and caches, when to use SQL vs NoSQL, cache eviction policies (LRU vs LFU), and the cache-aside pattern. Essential knowledge for system design interviews.
articles.author: System Design Sandbox

tableOfContents.title: The Core Concept: Different Tools for Different Jobs
SQL vs NoSQL: The Real Differences
The Biggest Mistake: Mixing Up Responsibilities
How This Looks in Real Systems
Summary: What to Remember for Interviews

articles.title: CAP Theorem
articles.subtitle: Understanding consistency, availability, and partition tolerance in distributed systems
articles.description: Learn the CAP theorem and how to make trade-offs between consistency, availability, and partition tolerance in distributed systems. Essential knowledge for system design interviews.
articles.author: System Design Sandbox

tableOfContents.title: The Core Idea: You Can't Have Everything
Why You Can't Have All Three
How to Talk About CAP in Interviews
The Reality: CAP is a Spectrum
Summary: What Actually Matters for Interviews

articles.title: Scaling: Vertical vs Horizontal
articles.subtitle: Understanding when to scale up vs scale out in distributed systems
articles.description: Learn the fundamental differences between vertical and horizontal scaling, when to use each approach, load balancers and API gateways, and how to make scaling decisions in system design interviews.
articles.author: System Design Sandbox

tableOfContents.title: The Two Ways to Scale: Bigger vs More
API Gateway / Load Balancer: The Traffic Cop
How to Talk About Scaling in Interviews

articles.title: Design a URL Shortener
articles.subtitle: Building a scalable URL shortening service like TinyURL or Bitly
articles.description: Learn how to design a production-ready URL shortening service. This guide covers pre-generated short codes, separating read and write servers, caching strategies, database replication, and scaling to handle millions of redirects per day.
articles.author: System Design Sandbox

articles.title: Object Storage & CDN
articles.subtitle: Understanding when to use object storage vs databases, and how CDNs accelerate content delivery
articles.description: Learn when to use object storage (S3) vs databases for content, how to separate metadata from content, and how CDNs cache data at edge locations. Essential for designing systems like Pastebin, Instagram, or any content-heavy service.
articles.author: System Design Sandbox

tableOfContents.title: The Core Concept: Separate Metadata from Content

articles.title: Design Pastebin
articles.subtitle: Building a scalable text paste sharing service
articles.description: Learn how to design a production-ready Pastebin service. This guide covers separating metadata from content using object storage, CDN integration for popular pastes, and scaling to handle millions of text snippets.
articles.author: System Design Sandbox

articles.title: Rate Limiting Algorithms
articles.subtitle: Understanding the algorithms behind rate limiting in distributed systems
articles.description: Learn the four main rate limiting algorithms (Fixed Window, Sliding Window Log, Sliding Window Counter, Token Bucket), when to use each, and how to implement distributed rate limiting with Redis. Essential for system design interviews.
articles.author: System Design Sandbox

tableOfContents.title: Where to Put the Rate Limiter

articles.title: Design a Distributed Rate Limiter
articles.subtitle: Building a scalable rate limiting service for distributed systems
articles.description: Learn how to design a production-ready distributed rate limiter. This guide covers algorithm selection, Redis-based distributed counting with Lua scripts, fail-open strategies, and scaling to handle millions of requests per second.
articles.author: System Design Sandbox

articles.title: Message Queues
articles.subtitle: Understanding asynchronous processing with Kafka and RabbitMQ
articles.description: Learn when and why to use message queues, the differences between Kafka and RabbitMQ, and key concepts like priority queues, dead letter queues, and at-least-once delivery. Essential for designing notification systems, order pipelines, and any async workflow.
articles.author: System Design Sandbox

tableOfContents.title: The Core Concept: Decouple Producers from Consumers
Kafka vs RabbitMQ: The Two Main Options
Key Concepts You Need to Know

articles.title: Design a Notification System
articles.subtitle: Building a scalable multi-channel notification service
articles.description: Learn how to design a production-ready notification system that sends millions of messages across Email, SMS, and Push channels. This guide covers message queues for async processing, priority-based delivery, user preference management, and retry strategies.
articles.author: System Design Sandbox

articles.title: WebSockets & Real-Time Communication
articles.subtitle: Understanding persistent connections for real-time features
articles.description: Learn how WebSockets enable real-time bidirectional communication, how they compare to HTTP polling and SSE, and how to scale stateful connections across servers. Essential for designing chat apps, live dashboards, and collaborative tools.
articles.author: System Design Sandbox

tableOfContents.title: HTTP vs WebSockets: The Fundamental Difference
The Polling Alternatives (and Why They Lose)
Presence Systems: Is the User Online?

articles.title: Design WhatsApp
articles.subtitle: Building a real-time chat application with presence and group messaging
articles.description: Learn how to design a production-ready chat application like WhatsApp. This guide covers WebSocket-based real-time messaging, Cassandra for write-heavy message storage, presence systems with Redis, and scaling stateful connections across servers.
articles.author: System Design Sandbox

articles.title: Idempotency & Deduplication
articles.subtitle: Preventing duplicate side effects in distributed systems
articles.description: Learn how idempotency keys and deduplication strategies prevent duplicate emails, double charges, and repeated webhook deliveries. Covers Redis SET NX, database unique constraints, and the at-least-once + idempotent consumer pattern.
articles.author: System Design Sandbox

tableOfContents.title: Why It Matters in Distributed Systems

articles.title: Fan-Out Strategies
articles.subtitle: Distributing events to many recipients at scale
articles.description: Learn fan-out-on-write vs fan-out-on-read, the celebrity problem, and the hybrid approach used by Twitter and Instagram. Covers feed generation, group chat delivery, notification fan-out, and webhook distribution.
articles.author: System Design Sandbox

tableOfContents.title: The Core Concept: One Event, Many Recipients

articles.title: Wide-Column Databases
articles.subtitle: Understanding Cassandra, partition keys, and write-heavy workloads
articles.description: Learn how wide-column databases like Cassandra organize data by partition key and clustering key, why LSM trees make writes fast, and how to design schemas query-first. Covers Cassandra vs DynamoDB vs MongoDB and when to choose each.
articles.author: System Design Sandbox

tableOfContents.title: Why Wide-Column Stores Excel at Write-Heavy Workloads
When NOT to Use Wide-Column Databases

articles.title: Redis Sorted Sets
articles.subtitle: How Redis keeps millions of scores ranked in real time
articles.description: Learn how Redis Sorted Sets (ZSET) power real-time leaderboards with O(log n) operations. Covers ZADD, ZRANGE, ZRANK commands, the skip list data structure underneath, handling ties, sharding sorted sets, and when to use alternatives.
articles.author: System Design Sandbox

tableOfContents.title: When a Single Redis Instance Isnt Enough

articles.title: Data Structures for System Design
articles.subtitle: The structures that actually show up in distributed systems interviews
articles.description: Learn the data structures that matter for system design: hash tables for caching, skip lists powering Redis, LSM trees in Cassandra, B-trees in PostgreSQL, and bloom filters for reducing disk reads. Not LeetCode -- system-level data structures.
articles.author: System Design Sandbox

tableOfContents.title: The Data Structures That Actually Come Up

articles.title: Sharding
articles.subtitle: Splitting data across machines when one database isn't enough
articles.description: Learn how to shard databases for scale: shard key selection, range-based vs hash-based partitioning, consistent hashing, cross-shard queries, rebalancing, and hotspot mitigation. Essential for designing systems with billions of rows.
articles.author: System Design Sandbox

articles.title: Microservices
articles.subtitle: When to split services and how to keep them talking
articles.description: Learn when microservices make sense vs a monolith, how to define service boundaries, sync vs async communication patterns, stateless services for horizontal scaling, service discovery, and the real downsides nobody warns you about.
articles.author: System Design Sandbox

articles.title: Design a Real-Time Leaderboard
articles.subtitle: Building a scalable leaderboard with Redis Sorted Sets
articles.description: Learn how to design a production-ready real-time leaderboard. This guide covers Redis Sorted Sets for O(log n) ranking, the skip list data structure underneath, dual-write persistence, handling ties with compound scores, and scaling to millions of players.
articles.author: System Design Sandbox

articles.title: Leader Election
articles.subtitle: Choosing one coordinator in a distributed system
articles.description: Learn how leader election works in distributed systems: ZooKeeper ephemeral nodes, etcd leases with Raft consensus, Redis-based elections, split brain prevention, and when to use leader election vs message queues. Essential for designing job schedulers, database replication, and distributed locks.
articles.author: System Design Sandbox

tableOfContents.title: etcd and Raft: The Modern Approach

articles.title: Cron Architecture
articles.subtitle: Running scheduled tasks reliably across distributed systems
articles.description: Learn how distributed cron systems work: exactly-once execution challenges, single leader polling with message queues, database row locking, partitioned scheduling, cron expression parsing, missed job handling, and real-world examples from Kubernetes and Airflow.
articles.author: System Design Sandbox

articles.title: Design a Distributed Job Scheduler
articles.subtitle: Building a reliable system for millions of scheduled tasks
articles.description: Learn how to design a production-ready distributed job scheduler. This guide covers leader election for coordinator failover, message queues for decoupled execution, pre-computed next_run_time for efficient polling, at-least-once delivery guarantees, and scaling to millions of scheduled jobs.
articles.author: System Design Sandbox

articles.title: Double-Entry Bookkeeping
articles.subtitle: The accounting pattern behind every reliable payment system
articles.description: Learn how double-entry bookkeeping ensures every payment is auditable and self-balancing. Covers debit/credit pairs, ledger immutability, the fundamental accounting equation, reconciliation, and when to use double-entry in system design interviews.
articles.author: System Design Sandbox

tableOfContents.title: Why It Matters in Payment Systems
Example: User Pays for an Order
When to Use Double-Entry (and When Not To)

articles.title: Distributed Transactions
articles.subtitle: Keeping data consistent across multiple services
articles.description: Learn how to coordinate transactions across microservices with Two-Phase Commit (2PC) and the Saga pattern. Covers compensating transactions, choreography vs orchestration, and when to use each approach in system design interviews.
articles.author: System Design Sandbox

tableOfContents.title: The Problem: Operations That Span Multiple Services
2PC vs Saga: When to Use Each

articles.title: Consistency Patterns
articles.subtitle: Choosing the right consistency model for each piece of data
articles.description: Learn the spectrum of consistency models from strong to eventual, practical patterns like read-your-writes and causal consistency, reconciliation strategies, and how to choose the right consistency level for financial data, social feeds, and user profiles.
articles.author: System Design Sandbox

tableOfContents.title: How to Choose: A Decision Framework

articles.title: Design a Payment System
articles.subtitle: Building an auditable payment service with PSP integration and double-entry ledger
articles.description: Learn how to design a production-ready payment system. This guide covers PSP integration (Stripe/PayPal), double-entry bookkeeping for audit trails, idempotency keys to prevent double charges, webhook handling, reconciliation batch jobs, and scaling financial infrastructure.
articles.author: System Design Sandbox
